query: What are the fees associated with each milestone in the ERCOT generation interconnection process based on ERCOT fee schedule and resource interconnection handbook?
final_answer:
Below is a milestone-by-milestone view of **ERCOT fees** you will encounter in the **generation interconnection (GINR) process**, using (i) the **ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective Oct. 1, 2024)** and (ii) the **ERCOT Resource Interconnection Handbook**.

## 1) Submit GINR / initiate Screening Study (Security Screening Study, “SSS”)
**Milestone (handbook):** The first step is submission of a GINR application; ERCOT performs the **Security Screening Study**. The handbook states the IE must pay an **ERCOT Security Screening Study fee**, and that the fee is **determined by the capacity** of the proposed resource and the IE should “refer to the current ERCOT Fee Schedule.”  
- **Fee (ERCOT Fee Schedule):** **Security Screening Study fee (capacity-based)** — the handbook confirms the fee exists and is capacity-tiered, but the specific tier-dollar amounts were **not present in the fee schedule pages available in the retrieved excerpts**.  
  - **Source (handbook):** Resource Interconnection Handbook (10/15/2014) describes the GINR + SSS step and states the SSS fee is capacity-based and refers to the ERCOT Fee Schedule.

## 2) Request / submit Full Interconnection Study (FIS) application
**Milestone:** After screening results, the IE requests the **Full Interconnection Study (FIS)**.

**Fee (ERCOT Fee Schedule):**
- **FIS Application Fee (new generator): $3,000**
- **FIS Application Fee (modification to existing generator): $2,700**  
  - **Source (fee schedule):** ERCOT Fee Schedule, effective **Oct. 1, 2024**, page showing “Full Interconnection Study (FIS) Application Fee”.

## 3) Execute interconnection agreement / build interconnection facilities (TSP-led)
**Milestone:** Negotiation/execution of interconnection agreement and construction of interconnection facilities.

**Fee treatment:** The ERCOT Fee Schedule excerpts provided do **not** specify a separate ERCOT “milestone fee” for IA negotiation or construction. In ERCOT, much of the cost in this phase is typically **TSP engineering/design/construction and network upgrade costs** handled through the interconnection agreement and TSP processes (not an ERCOT-administered user fee itemized in the fee schedule excerpts we pulled).

## 4) Register the generator with ERCOT (Resource registration / qualification step)
As the project approaches commercial readiness, you encounter **ERCOT generator registration / modification fees**.

**Fees (ERCOT Fee Schedule):**
- **Generator registration fee (generator < 10 MW): $8,000**
- **Generator registration fee (generator ≥ 10 MW): $14,000**
- **Settlement Only Distribution Generator (SODG) registration: $2,300**  
- **Change existing SODG to a DGR: $8,000**  
  - **Source (fee schedule):** ERCOT Fee Schedule, effective **Oct. 1, 2024**, “Load Resource Registration and Generator Interconnection or Modification Fees” section.

### Generator modification fees (for changes to an existing generator)
The fee schedule also specifies (in the excerpted pages available) modification fees tied to the **cumulative MW amount of changes submitted in the prior 12 months**:
- **If modifications are < 10 MW:** **$2,300**
- **If modifications are ≥ 10 MW:** **$14,000**  
  - **Source (fee schedule):** ERCOT Fee Schedule, effective **Oct. 1, 2024**, page showing modification fee thresholds (<10 MW vs ≥10 MW).

## 5) Market participation entity set-up (as applicable to your commercialization path)
These are not strictly “engineering interconnection milestones,” but they are commonly triggered during late-stage development/commissioning when entities are established in ERCOT systems.

**Fees (ERCOT Fee Schedule):**
- **$500 per entity** for each of the following registrations (if/when applicable):  
  **QSE, Sub-QSE, CR, CRR Account Holder, IMRE, Resource Entity, TDSP**  
  - **Source (fee schedule):** ERCOT Fee Schedule, effective **Oct. 1, 2024**, page listing $500 per entity for these roles.

## 6) Weatherization inspections (compliance/operational readiness – as applicable)
The fee schedule includes inspection-related fees that can arise as a project becomes operational.

**Fees (ERCOT Fee Schedule excerpts):**
- **TSP weatherization inspection:** **$4,500 per inspected substation or switching station** (paid by TSPs)
- **Resource Entity weatherization inspection fees:** assessed/allocated based on MW share (calculated semiannually per the fee schedule description)  
  - **Source (fee schedule):** ERCOT Fee Schedule, effective **Oct. 1, 2024**, inspection fee page.

---

# Summary table (what you can directly price from the retrieved fee schedule excerpts)

| Interconnection milestone | Fee item (ERCOT) | Amount (per ERCOT Fee Schedule excerpts) |
|---|---|---|
| GINR submission / Screening Study (SSS) | Security Screening Study fee | **Capacity-based** (handbook references fee schedule; $ tiers not visible in retrieved excerpt pages) |
| FIS request / application | FIS Application Fee | **$3,000 (new)** / **$2,700 (modification)** |
| Resource registration (generator) | Generator registration | **$8,000 (<10 MW)** / **$14,000 (≥10 MW)** |
| Generator modifications | Generator modification fee | **$2,300 (<10 MW cumulative changes/12 months)** / **$14,000 (≥10 MW)** |
| Entity registrations (if applicable) | QSE / RE / etc. | **$500 per entity** |
| Weatherization inspection (if applicable) | TSP inspection | **$4,500 per substation/switching station** |

---

## Important caveat (so you don’t mis-budget)
The **big-ticket costs** in ERCOT interconnection are often **TSP-managed** (engineering, station work, line work, protection & control, telecom, etc.). Those are typically **not** “ERCOT user fees” listed in the ERCOT Fee Schedule, and therefore don’t appear as fixed ERCOT milestone fees above.

If you need, I can also format this into an “interconnection budget checklist” separating **(A) ERCOT fees** vs **(B) TSP/3rd-party study & construction costs**—but the ERCOT fixed-fee items above are the ones explicitly visible from the fee schedule excerpts and/or directly referenced by the handbook.
